# Benchmark Claude {.unnumbered} 

**DOCX Multi-Format Pipeline\
Test Document**

*A comprehensive specimen of word-processor features\
for conversion pipeline validation*

Version 1.0 \| 2026-05-20 \| CC BY 4.0

# Contents

(Right-click → Update Field to populate)

# 1 Typography & Inline Formatting

## 1.1 Heading Level 2

### 1.1.1 Heading Level 3

#### 1.1.1.1 Heading Level 4

This paragraph demonstrates standard body text. **Bold text.** *Italic text. **Bold-italic text. ***[Underlined text.]{.underline} ~~Strikethrough.~~ Double-strikethrough.

[Small-caps run.]{.smallcaps} All-caps run.

Chemical formula: H~2~O \| Footnote reference^1^ (see footnote).[^1]

Colour palette: **Red Green Blue Teal**

Highlights: [Yellow Cyan Green Magenta DarkRed]{.mark}

Font sizes: 8pt 9pt 10pt 11pt 12pt 14pt 16pt 18pt 24pt

Typefaces: Arial Times New Roman Courier New Georgia Calibri

## 1.2 Line & Paragraph Spacing

Single line spacing --- Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit, sed do eiusmod tempor.

1.5 line spacing --- Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit, sed do eiusmod tempor.

Double line spacing --- Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit, sed do eiusmod tempor.

## 1.3 Paragraph Alignment

Left-aligned text.

Centre-aligned text.

Right-aligned text.

Justified text --- Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit, sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua.

## 1.4 Indentation & Block Quote

> This paragraph has a left indent of 1 inch and first-line indent of −0.5 in (hanging indent).
>
> *\"The greatest shortcoming of the human race is our inability to understand the exponential function.\"\
> --- Albert Bartlett*

# 2 Lists

## 2.1 Unordered (Bullet) List

-   First bullet item

-   Second bullet item with sub-items:

<!-- -->

-   Sub-item A

-   Sub-item B

<!-- -->

-   Sub-sub-item i

-   Sub-sub-item ii

<!-- -->

-   Third bullet item

## 2.2 Ordered (Numbered) List

1.  First numbered item

2.  Second numbered item

<!-- -->

1.  Sub-numbered item a

2.  Sub-numbered item b

<!-- -->

3.  Third numbered item

## 2.3 Definition-style List

> **Term 1:** Definition of term 1 --- an explanation of what it means.
>
> **Term 2:** Definition of term 2 --- another explanation.

# 3 Tables

## 3.1 Basic Table with Header Row

  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Country**       **Capital**       **Population (M)**   **GDP (USD tn)**
  ----------------- ----------------- -------------------- ------------------
  Germany           Berlin            83.2                 4.1

  France            Paris             67.4                 2.9

  United Kingdom    London            67.2                 3.1

  Spain             Madrid            47.4                 1.4
  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 3.2 Table with Merged Cells

  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Merged Header spanning all three columns**                           
  ---------------------------------------------- ----------------------- -----------------------
  Row-merged cell                                R1 C2                   R1 C3

                                                 R2 C2                   R2 C3
  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 4 Code & Pre-formatted Text

## 4.1 Inline Code

Use the function calculate_p_value(data, alpha=0.05) to compute significance.

## 4.2 Code Block

> def fibonacci(n: int) -\> int:
>
> \"\"\"Return the nth Fibonacci number.\"\"\"
>
> if n \<= 1:
>
> return n
>
> return fibonacci(n - 1) + fibonacci(n - 2)
>
> \# Example
>
> for i in range(10):
>
> print(f\'F({i}) = {fibonacci(i)}\')

# 5 Hyperlinks & Cross-references

External hyperlink: [W3C WCAG 2.1 Guidelines](https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG21/).

Internal cross-reference: see section 1 Typography & Inline Formatting

# 6 Mathematical Equations

The Pythagorean theorem expressed as an OMML equation:

$$x2^{}\text{+}\text{ }y2^{}\text{=}\text{ }z2^{}$$

Inline mathematical notation (plain text fallback): E = mc², where c ≈ 2.998 × 10⁸ m/s.

# 7 Special Characters & Symbols

Typographic: 'single quotes' "double quotes" -- en-dash --- em-dash ... ellipsis © ® ™ ¼ ½ ¾ ° µ ¶ † ‡ ← → ↔ ⇒ ∀ ∃ ∈ ∞ √ ∫

Non-breaking space between words.

Soft hyphen: dis­crimination (hyphen should only appear at line break).

# 8 Figures & Captions

![](img/ch10/media/image1.png){width="3.0in" height="1.5in"}

*Figure: Cornflower Blue -- Figure 1*

![](img/ch10/media/image2.png){width="3.0in" height="1.5in"}

*Figure: Medium Sea Green -- Figure 2*

![](img/ch10/media/image3.png){width="3.0in" height="1.5in"}

*Figure: Orange -- Figure 3*

# 9 Section Dividers

Paragraph before horizontal rule.

Paragraph after horizontal rule.

A hard page break follows:

# 10 Multi-column Layout

Column text paragraph 1 --- Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit, sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua. Ut enim ad minim veniam, quis nostrud exercitation ullamco laboris.

Column text paragraph 2 --- Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit, sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua. Ut enim ad minim veniam, quis nostrud exercitation ullamco laboris.

Column text paragraph 3 --- Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit, sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua. Ut enim ad minim veniam, quis nostrud exercitation ullamco laboris.

Column text paragraph 4 --- Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit, sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua. Ut enim ad minim veniam, quis nostrud exercitation ullamco laboris.

# 11 Built-in Styles Showcase

> This paragraph uses the \'Quote\' built-in style.
>
> This paragraph uses the \'Intense Quote\' built-in style.

This paragraph uses the \'No Spacing\' style with no extra spacing between lines.

# 12 Track Changes & Comments (markers)

This paragraph contains text that would typically be subject to tracked changes review. In a full pipeline test, insertions and deletions should survive round-trips.

Normal text followed by inserted text (tracked) and more normal text.

Normal text followed by and continuation.

[^1]: This is footnote 1 --- water is H₂O, the most abundant compound on Earth.
